prerequisites: A Chargebee account with an active site (the site name is the subdomain in your Chargebee URL; for example if your dashboard URL is https://acme.chargebee.com, your site name is acme).
A Chargebee API key. API keys are environment-specific: use the test site key for development and the live site key for production.
Access to your Chargebee admin console to create or retrieve an API key.
Knowledge of your site's Product Catalog version (1.0 or 2.0). Sites created after May 5, 2021 use Product Catalog 2.0 by default; older sites use 1.0.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic authentication)
authMethods.summary: Chargebee uses HTTP Basic authentication for API calls. The username is your API key and the password is empty. The connector takes this value in the secret field site_api_key and pairs it with the site subdomain. API keys are different for your test site and your live site.

setupSteps.title: Create or retrieve a Chargebee API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to the Chargebee admin console and obtain an API key. Store the key securely. Use the test site key for development and the live site key for production.

setupSteps.title: Identify your site name
setupSteps.body: Set the required site field to your Chargebee site name, which is the subdomain of your Chargebee URL. For example, if your dashboard is at https://acme.chargebee.com, the site value is acme.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key as the site_api_key secret
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key into the required, secret site_api_key field. The connector sends it as the HTTP Basic auth username with an empty password on every request.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field using the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ (for example 2021-01-25T00:00:00Z). Only data created or updated on or after this date is replicated.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional fields
setupSteps.body: Optionally set product_catalog to match your Chargebee site's Product Catalog version (1.0 or 2.0; defaults to 2.0). Optionally set num_workers to control the number of concurrent threads used during sync.
